title: pygeum subcordatum
intro: Pygeum subcordatum, also known as the African cherry or Prunus africana, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating urinary issues such as benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH), due to its ability to inhibit the growth of prostate cells. The health benefits of Pygeum subcordatum include reducing symptoms of BPH, improving urinary flow, and alleviating lower back pain. Pygeum subcordatum can be prepared as herbal extracts, capsules, or tablets, often standardized to contain specific amounts of the key compounds, including ferulic acid and ursolic acid.
